source.title: Crane Boom Collapse - Talos ERT LLC OCS-G02280 South Marsh Island 130 B Facility

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - South Marsh Island Block 130

actions_taken: BSEE onsite investigation conducted on May 15, 2023
Four samples of failed boom cable sent to Core Lifting for analysis and testing on July 20, 2023
Break tests conducted on 12-foot sections of failed cable
Bridle assembly inspected by Sparrows for non-biased assessment
Boom cable from same spool tested on another Talos crane and passed
Sheaves visually inspected and bridle replaced
event.summary: On May 12, 2023, at approximately 1605 hours, a crane boom collapse occurred on the Talos ERT LLC OCS-G02280 South Marsh Island 130 B Facility. While attempting to lower a basket of grating weighing approximately 11,000 lbs to the top deck using a four-part load line, the boom cable failed when the grating rack was approximately 6 ft from the top deck and the boom angle was at 76 degrees. The boom collapsed and landed on the top deck, causing approximately $330,000 in damage to the deck and production equipment and $370,000 in crane damage. There were no injuries to personnel. Analysis by Core Lifting determined the boom cable failed due to fatigue from numerous cycles through the bridle sheaves, evidenced by numerous short pieces of wire embedded in the cable with fatigue fractures at both ends. The crane had accumulated 124 hours of run time since the boom cable was installed on May 28, 2021.
recommendations: Replace wire rope when dimension reaches nominal wire rope size, not reduction size as per API RP 2D
Include additional wire rope micrometer evaluations of boom cable on all Talos operated cranes during quarterly and annual inspection schedules, with readings taken in areas of high rotation and wear
Conduct boom cable replacement at maximum of 100 hours or within one year on SMI-130B crane to determine adequate wire rope replacement criteria and crane function
Search for alternative lubrication that will provide ample penetration to cable core
key_phrases: confined area of boom cable through bridle sheaves
wire rope evaluations failed to recognize fatigue
Lubriplate lubrication failed to penetrate core

threats.name: Wire Rope Fatigue from Cyclic Loading

controls.name: Annual Crane Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Detection and verification of crane component integrity
controls.lod_basis: Periodic inspection to detect degradation before failure

supporting_text: According to the last annual crane inspection that was conducted on May 4, 2023, the boom cable was installed on May 28, 2021, with a breaking strength of 51,000 lbs.
The .645 caliper diameter of the boom cable recorded on the annual inspections between installation and the cable failure had not changed.
Also, there were no discrepancies found with the boom cable on the last annual crane inspection.
Equipment Failure – Inadequate preventative maintenance: wire rope evaluations failed to recognize the fatigue occurring in the confined area of the boom cable.

controls.name: Wire Rope Diameter Measurement
controls.barrier_role: Detection of wire rope degradation through dimensional changes

supporting_text: The .645 caliper diameter of the boom cable recorded on the annual inspections between installation and the cable failure had not changed.
wire rope evaluations failed to recognize the fatigue occurring in the confined area of the boom cable.

Include additional wire rope micrometer evaluations of the boom cable on all Talos operated cranes during quarterly and annual inspection schedules. Readings will be taken in areas of high rotation and wear.

controls.name: Wire Rope Lubrication
controls.barrier_role: Reduce friction and wear on wire rope

supporting_text: it was noted during the analysis that the 'Lubriplate' lubrication used on the boom cable failed to penetrate the core of the cable.

During the analysis, it was found that the 'Lubriplate' lubrication used on the boom cable failed to penetrate the core of the cable. Talos is searching for an alternative lubrication that will provide ample penetration to the cable core.

controls.name: Crane Pull Test
controls.barrier_role: Verification of crane system load capacity
controls.lod_basis: Periodic testing to verify system integrity

supporting_text: A pull test of 35,200 lbs. was conducted during the annual inspection.

controls.name: Wire Rope Replacement Criteria (API RP 2D)
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control for component lifecycle management

supporting_text: Talos will replace wire rope when the dimension of the wire rope reaches its nominal wire rope size and not the reduction size as per API RP 2D (Sixth Edition) G.5.1.3 - 8 Wire Rope Replacement Criteria.
Conduct boom cable replacement at a maximum of 100 hours or within one year on the SMI-130B crane to determine adequate wire rope replacement criteria and crane function.

controls.name: Sheave and Bridle Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Detection of wear points contributing to rope fatigue

supporting_text: The sheaves are 13" in diameter by 5/8" and were visually inspected when the boom cable was removed. Even though there was no damage to the sheaves, the bridle was replaced.

BSEE requested an inspection of the bridle assembly as well as the results of the inspection. Talos sent the bridle to Sparrows to ensure a non-biased inspection. The results concluded that it did not appear that the bridle directly contributed to the boom cable failure.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Pre-task hazard identification and control planning
controls.lod_basis: Planning control to identify and mitigate hazards

supporting_text: The AI requested additional information pertaining to the incident such as the Job Safety Analysis (JSA), crane inspections, crane lifting procedure, statements, and other relevant documents from Talos.

controls.name: Crane Lifting Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Operational control for safe lifting operations

supporting_text: The AI requested additional information pertaining to the incident such as the Job Safety Analysis (JSA), crane inspections, crane lifting procedure, statements, and other relevant documents from Talos.

controls.name: Personnel Exclusion Zone During Lifting
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel injury from dropped loads or structural collapse

supporting_text: There were no injuries to personnel.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
